item.title: Sensex and Nifty Slump as Market Breadth Deteriorates Sharply
item.description: Indian equity markets witnessed a pronounced sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging nearly 2% and the Nifty shedding over 488 points. Market breadth deteriorated sharply as all sectors declined, led by metal stocks, while mid and small caps dragged the broader indices lower amid subdued investor sentiment and cautious foreign institutional investor activity. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Broad-Based Decline Amid Weak Market Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced notable weakness recently, declining by 2.67% on the day and 1.32% over the past five trading sessions. Despite this overall downturn, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape amid challenging market conditions. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, has experienced a notable decline, retreating by 2.61% on the day and 2.36% over the past five trading sessions. Despite this downturn, certain stocks within the segment have demonstrated resilience and even upward momentum, reflecting a complex market landscape marked by sectoral disparities and selective upgrades. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Stock Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment has experienced a notable downturn, with the BSE 100 index falling 2.15% today and a sharper 3.43% decline over the past five days. Despite this broad weakness, individual stock performances reveal a complex interplay between defensive resilience and cyclical pressures, underscoring the challenges facing investors in this market phase. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Sensex Slumps Nearly 2% as All Sectors Decline; Metal Sector Leads Losses
item.description: The Indian equity market witnessed a broad-based sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1.89% to close at 74,600.74 amid widespread sectoral weakness and subdued investor sentiment. Market breadth was severely negative as all 38 sectors declined, led by a sharp fall in metals, while foreign institutional investors remained net sellers. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining by 2.07% on the day and registering a sharper 3.36% fall over the past five trading sessions. This performance reflects a broad-based weakness with a stark advance-decline ratio of just 0.03x, as 97 stocks declined against only 3 advancing, underscoring the prevailing bearish sentiment among heavyweight constituents. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, experienced a notable decline on 13 Mar 2026, reflecting broader market pressures. The index fell by 2.54% on the day and has declined 2.29% over the past five trading sessions, signalling a challenging environment for mid-sized companies amid sectoral headwinds and weak market breadth. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Downward Pressure Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced a notable decline, falling by 2.47% on the day and registering a 1.12% drop over the past five trading sessions. Despite this recent weakness, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape amid broad market pressures. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Sensex Drops Over 1500 Points as Market Sentiment Turns Bearish Across Sectors
item.description: Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1,529.10 points or 2.01% to close at 74,505.32. The decline was broad-based, with all 38 sectors on the BSE ending in the red and market breadth severely skewed towards declines. Mid and small caps also suffered heavy losses, signalling widespread risk aversion among investors amid weak global cues and subdued domestic sentiment. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Broad-Based Decline Amid Weak Market Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, as represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced notable weakness recently, declining by 2.85% on the day and 1.5% over the past five trading sessions. This downturn reflects broader market pressures and sector-specific challenges, with a significant majority of stocks in the segment retreating amid subdued investor sentiment. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining by 2.81% on the day and registering a 2.55% drop over the past five trading sessions. Despite this broad weakness, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape driven by sectoral disparities and uneven market breadth. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, has experienced a notable downturn, declining 2.25% on the day and 3.54% over the past five trading sessions. This broad-based weakness is underscored by a stark advance-decline ratio, with only 4 stocks advancing against 96 declining, signalling widespread selling pressure. Within this challenging environment, Tata Consumer Products emerged as the best performer with a modest gain of 1.28%, while Larsen & Toubro (L&T) lagged significantly, falling 7.01% and marking the segment’s worst performance. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Sensex Drops 1.6% as Market Sentiment Turns Bearish; Metals Sector Leads Declines
item.description: The Indian equity markets witnessed a sharp sell-off on 13 Mar 2026, with the Sensex plunging 1.60% to close at 74,820.65, extending losses throughout the session. Market breadth was overwhelmingly negative as all 37 sectors declined, led by a steep fall in the metals segment. Large caps traded largely flat with select exceptions, while mid and small caps bore the brunt of the downturn amid subdued global cues and cautious investor sentiment. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Faces Broad Decline Amid Defensive and Cyclical Stock Divergence
item.description: The large-cap segment witnessed a pronounced downturn on 13 Mar 2026, with the BSE 100 index declining by 1.84% on the day and a sharper 3.13% drop over the past five sessions. This broad-based weakness was underscored by a lopsided advance-decline ratio, where only 6 stocks advanced against 94 decliners, signalling pervasive selling pressure. Notably, Tata Consumer emerged as the best performer with a modest 2.26% gain, while Larsen & Toubro (L&T) lagged significantly, falling 5.68% despite a recent upgrade in its technical rating. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Faces Broad-Based Decline Amid Weak Market Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has experienced a notable decline, falling by 2.55% on the day and registering a 1.2% drop over the past five trading sessions. This downturn reflects a broader market correction, with sectoral performances and breadth indicators signalling a cautious outlook for investors in this space. Time: 5 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Faces Sharp Decline Amid Broad Market Weakness
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, has experienced notable weakness over recent sessions, declining by 2.46% on the day and 2.21% over the past five days. Despite this broad downturn, select stocks within the segment have bucked the trend, highlighting a mixed performance landscape amid challenging market conditions. Time: 5 hours ago
